# Restaurant Menu & Ordering Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-restaurant-restaurant-menu-site
**Vertical slug:** restaurant
**VN-ID:** VN-FOD-001
**Niche family:** NF-FOD-QSR (anchor)
**Family role:** anchor
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria's food service sector is worth over ₦1.4 trillion and growing, with street-food culture embedded in daily life across all 36 states. Small and medium restaurants (buka, mama-put, fast food joints, canteens) are the dominant format — mostly owner-operated, serving local dishes (jollof rice, egusi, pepper soup, suya, pounded yam). Digital presence is rare; most customer discovery happens via referrals, roadside boards, and Instagram. WhatsApp is the universal ordering channel. NAFDAC food business registration is legally required for prepared food sales.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Chinyere, 34, Abuja civil servant**: Wants to pre-order jollof rice and chicken for office lunch via WhatsApp so it is ready for collection. Converts on seeing menu with prices and a one-tap WA link.
- **Emeka, 28, Lagos Mainland resident**: Seeks a local restaurant for weekend family suya and assorted pepper soup. Needs menu photos, price list, and location before deciding.
- **Ngozi, 45, Port Harcourt market trader**: Regular customer seeking canteen that offers affordable daily specials. Calls or WhatsApps to confirm availability.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home**: Hero (restaurant name, cuisine type), WhatsApp order CTA, featured menu (6 items with prices), trust strip (NAFDAC, hygiene cert), about blurb, contact strip
- **About**: Restaurant story, team, hygiene standards, NAFDAC registration, payment methods, WhatsApp button
- **Services (Menu)**: Full menu grid with item name, description, ₦ price; WhatsApp order CTA at bottom
- **Contact**: WhatsApp block, location, hours, enquiry form (NDPR-compliant)

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Warm, welcoming, appetite-stimulating. "Taste of Home", "Fresh Daily", "Order Now via WhatsApp" language. Never overly formal. Mention Nigerian dishes by name — jollof, egusi, pounded yam, suya, moi moi. Avoid western fast food language. Primary CTA: "Order via WhatsApp". Secondary: "See Full Menu".

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- NAFDAC food business registration number displayed
- CAC registration
- Hygiene/food handler certification
- Years in business
- Customer testimonials (Nigerian names: Blessing, Tunde, Amara)
- Payment methods: Cash, POS, bank transfer, Paystack

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Steaming pot of jollof rice being served — Lagos buka counter, colourful setting
2. Plate of assorted pepper soup with goat meat — restaurant table, wooden surface
3. Suya being grilled on open coal grill — night market, Mallam grilling suya
4. Pounded yam and egusi soup — beautifully plated, earthenware bowl
5. Restaurant owner (Nigerian woman in apron) smiling in front of her kitchen
6. Group of office workers having lunch — canteen/restaurant setting, Lagos
7. WhatsApp order confirmation screenshot — showing customer message and response

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- [ ] NAFDAC food business registration number displayed
- [ ] CAC registration number visible
- [ ] Hygiene certification displayed
- [ ] NDPR consent on contact form
- [ ] No health claims without regulatory basis

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp CTA on home and contact pages
- [x] All prices in ₦ (Naira)
- [x] Nigerian city names used (Lagos, Abuja, PH)
- [x] Nigerian dish names in menu
- [x] Nigerian names in testimonials
- [x] NAFDAC registration trust signal
- [x] Paystack / bank transfer / POS / cash payment methods
- [x] Mobile-first layout (375px min)
- [x] esc() on all user inputs
- [x] NDPR consent notice on contact form
